items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'cooking food briefly in boiling water then cooling'? (set 2)
items.explanation: cooking food briefly in boiling water then cooling corresponds to blanching.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'base mixture of chopped onion, carrot, and celery'? (set 2)
items.explanation: base mixture of chopped onion, carrot, and celery corresponds to mirepoix.

items.q: In kitchen basics, what is 'the browning reaction on meat and bread'?
items.explanation: The expected answer is Maillard reaction.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'to cut ingredients into very small cubes'? (set 2)
items.explanation: to cut ingredients into very small cubes corresponds to brunoise.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'boiling water at sea level'? (set 2)
items.explanation: boiling water at sea level corresponds to 100C.

items.q: Overcrowding a pan can reduce browning.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'the browning reaction on meat and bread'? (set 2)
items.explanation: the browning reaction on meat and bread corresponds to Maillard reaction.

items.q: Blanching usually includes a quick ice bath after boiling.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'French term for place and prep before service'? (set 2)
items.explanation: French term for place and prep before service corresponds to mise en place.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'resting cooked meat does what'? (set 2)
items.explanation: resting cooked meat does what corresponds to redistributes juices.
